summary: First implementation testing completed. Backend APIs 93.5% pass rate (29/31 tests). Frontend loads correctly but login/register navigation broken - users cannot authenticate through UI. Core backend functionality verified working.

minor.issue: Duplicate email test failed - returned 200 instead of 400. Test fixture issue, not critical bug.
AI continue session test failed with JSONDecodeError. AI integration timing issue, first call works.

integration_issues.issue: CRITICAL: Login and register forms submit but do not navigate to next screen. User stays on auth page with no error message. Backend API returns 200 OK with valid token, but frontend does not proceed. Possible AsyncStorage issue in web preview or navigation not triggering after auth state update.

issues: Console warning: shadow* style props deprecated, use boxShadow (non-blocking)

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix frontend auth flow - login/register buttons do not navigate after successful API response. Check AuthContext login/register functions and index.tsx navigation logic. Verify AsyncStorage works in web environment or switch to localStorage for web.
Investigate why Expo Router navigation doesn't trigger after auth state changes in AuthContext
Test auth flow in actual Expo Go app (not just web preview) to isolate if issue is web-specific
Add error handling and user feedback if AsyncStorage.setItem fails
Add loading spinner visibility during auth API calls
Fix AI session continue endpoint to handle edge cases better
critical_code_review_comments: AuthContext: After login/register, token is saved to AsyncStorage and user state is set, but navigation may not trigger. Index.tsx should redirect based on user state, but this appears not to work.
api.ts uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL correctly, health check from browser works, so base URL is correct
Login.tsx and Register.tsx handle errors but may swallow navigation errors silently
Backend server.py is 878 lines - approaching 700 line recommended limit, consider splitting into modules
All backend endpoints properly exclude MongoDB _id from responses
All interactive UI elements have proper testID attributes
seed_data_creation: Test user created: test_unicorn_001@example.com (onboarded=true) with profile, goal, habit, daily entries, wormhole contacts
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully functional and tested. Frontend loads but authentication flow is broken - users cannot login or register through UI. After fixing auth flow, need to test: full onboarding (4 steps), Today page interactions (determination slider, five core actions, compound habit), date navigation, AI chat, Wormhole contacts, Dashboard, Profile editing. All backend APIs work correctly via direct HTTP calls.

rca_of_issue.issue: Login and Register buttons do not navigate after form submission
reproduction_steps: 2. Fill login form with test_unicorn_001@example.com / testpass123456
4. Observe: Button shows loading briefly, then returns to normal. No navigation occurs, no error shown.
rca_of_issue.root_cause_analysis: Backend API returns 200 OK with valid token (verified via curl and pytest). The issue is in frontend: (1) AsyncStorage may not work properly in Expo web preview, OR (2) Navigation in index.tsx not triggering after AuthContext state update, OR (3) API call error being swallowed silently. Most likely: AsyncStorage.setItem succeeds but doesn't trigger useEffect in index.tsx that checks auth state and performs navigation.
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Add explicit navigation after successful auth in AuthContext login/register functions. Use router.replace() directly after setting token and user state. For web, consider using localStorage instead of AsyncStorage. Add try-catch with console.error around AsyncStorage calls to catch any silent failures.
